name: Bash deploy script with rollback on failure
setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval_deploy && printf '#!/bin/bash
# Healthcheck: succeeds when current_version is 1.0, fails for 2.0
VER=$(cat /tmp/eval_deploy/current_version 2>/dev/null)
[[ "$VER" == "1.0" ]] && exit 0 || exit 1
' > /tmp/eval_deploy/healthcheck.sh && chmod +x /tmp/eval_deploy/healthcheck.sh && echo 1.0 > /tmp/eval_deploy/current_version
task: Create /tmp/eval_deploy/deploy.sh that takes a version string as its first argument and: (1) saves the current version to /tmp/eval_deploy/previous_version, (2) writes the new version to /tmp/eval_deploy/current_version, (3) runs /tmp/eval_deploy/healthcheck.sh; (4) if the healthcheck exits non-zero, restore current_version from previous_version and exit non-zero; (5) on success exit 0. Make the script executable.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_deploy; chmod +x ./deploy.sh 2>/dev/null || true; [[ -x ./deploy.sh ]] || { echo 'deploy.sh not executable'; exit 1; }; ./deploy.sh 1.0 || { echo 'initial deploy 1.0 failed'; exit 1; }; [[ "$(cat current_version)" == '1.0' ]] || { echo 'expected 1.0'; exit 1; }; if ./deploy.sh 2.0; then echo 'should have failed for 2.0'; exit 1; fi; [[ "$(cat current_version)" == '1.0' ]] || { echo "rollback failed; got $(cat current_version)"; exit 1; }; echo OK
